stories.title: Explosives found near pipeline in Serbia probably ‘Russian provocation’, says expert
stories.summary: Former Ukrainian major general says 4kg of material was most likely an attempt to influence Hungary’s election Hungary elections explainer The amount of explosives discovered in Serbia last week would not have been enough to destroy the Balkan Stream gas pipeline, prompting an expert to conclude it was probably a Russian intelligence plot aimed at influencing Hungary’s impending election. A former Ukrainian major general and a munitions specialist told the Guardian calculations made by his compa

stories.title: Key Democrats call on Eric Swalwell to end campaign for California governor
stories.summary: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries and former Speaker Nancy Pelosi headlined a growing list of Democratic lawmakers called on Rep. Eric Swalwell Friday to withdraw his campaign for California governor amid  allegations of sexual misconduct . “This extremely sensitive matter must be appropriately investigated with full transparency and accountability,” Pelosi said in a statement. “As I discussed with Congressman Swalwell, it is clear that is best done outside of a gubernatorial campaign.”

stories.title: Trump promises economic support to Orbán ahead of crucial Hungary vote
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump promised to give economic support to Hungary if needed by Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, reiterating his backing for the Hungarian leader just hours ahead of a crucial election in the country. “My Administration stands ready to use the full Economic Might of the United States to strengthen Hungary’s Economy, as we have done for our Great Allies in the past, if Prime Minister Viktor Orbán and the Hungarian People ever need it,” Trump wrote in a post on Truth Social ahead

stories.title: ‘He cares about Hungarians’: the small Ukrainian town divided over Orbán
stories.summary: The rightwing populist’s support for the majority-Hungarian population of Berehove means they may offer their votes in return Hungary elections: what is at stake and who is likely to win? Across much of Ukraine, Sunday’s parliamentary election in Hungary is being followed with a singular hope: that Viktor Orbán, the Kremlin-friendly leader who has made opposition to Kyiv a centrepiece of his campaign, will be voted out after 16 years in office. But in Berehove, the mood is more complicated. Cont

stories.title: Zebras, wealth and power: Hungary’s election tests Orbán’s grip on power
stories.summary: Corruption scandals and a surging opposition have turned the vote into the biggest test yet for the long-serving populist leader The drone footage showed a sprawling residence in northern Hungary, complete with manicured gardens, a swimming pool and an underground garage. But it was what came next that captured much of the country’s imagination: zebras darting across the countryside. Continue reading...

stories.title: Swedish exhibition explores life of 18th-century Black diarist
stories.summary: Born into slavery, Adolf Ludvig Couschi Badin became part of Swedish royal court and left legacy of books and letters In 1760, a Black child around 10 years old arrived at the Swedish royal court as a “gift” to the queen. Adolf Ludvig Gustav Fredrik Albrecht Couschi, who became known as Badin (derived from the French for joker or prankster), later held titles including chamberlain, court secretary, ballet master and civil servant. He is thought to have been born into slavery between 1747 and 175

stories.title: Hungarians speak to the Guardian before decisive election – video
stories.summary: Voters will head to the polls on Sunday in what is seen as Europe’s most consequential election of the year. The Guardian's Jakub Krupa and Flora Garamvolgyi speak to Hungarians in Budapest before the vote Continue reading...

stories.title: An environmental disaster in Moldova has Russia’s fingerprints all over it | Paula Erizanu
stories.summary: The Ukraine war on our doorstep is a constant threat. Contaminated drinking water is a dangerous new twist In the second week of March, the nature vlogger Ilie Cojocari went out to film the arrival of spring on the Nistru (Dniester) river, 70 metres away from his home in Naslavcea, a village bordering Ukraine on the northernmost point of Moldova. But as he approached the river he could smell the stench of oil rising up from the water and see dark spots floating on its surface. Something was wron

stories.title: As Hungary gears up for a historic election, DW answers a few key questions
stories.summary: Hungary's election on April 12 is the most important for the state since 1989, and also of historic significance for the EU. DW takes a more detailed look.

stories.title: Hungary: Scandal, fear and hope ahead of vote
stories.summary: Fake news and a growing number of political scandals: Hungary hasn't seen an election this fraught since after 1989's collapse of the Soviet Union.

stories.title: Hungary Elections: There is hope that Peter Magyar can deliver a new beginning
stories.summary: What will Hungary’s elections bring? In an op-ed for Euronews, EU politician Valérie Hayer expresses hope that Péter Magyar could usher in a new political beginning, while warning that another term for Viktor Orbán risks cementing irreversible democratic backsliding.

stories.title: Final push for votes as challenger to Hungary's Orbán senses victory
stories.summary: As Péter Magyar's opposition movement leads in the polls, tens of thousands of anti-Orbán supporters fill Heroes' Square in Budapest.

stories.title: Hungary Election: An Orbán victory would be good news for Europe
stories.summary: Hungary elections: An Orbán victory would be good news for Europe, writes Gerolf Annemans of the “Patriots for Europe” group in the European Parliament in an op-ed for Euronews. Annemans says Orbán has become a symbol of resistance to centralisation.

stories.title: Human rights groups decry US plan for Guantánamo camp for Cuban migrants
stories.summary: Exclusive: Dozens of organizations write to Congress after general announced plan to ‘deal with’ those fleeing any humanitarian crisis on the island Dozens of US and international human rights organizations are decrying the Trump administration ’s plans to establish a migrant “camp” for fleeing Cubans at the Guantánamo Bay military base if the island nation’s crisis worsens under pressure from the US, according to a letter to members of Congress on Friday. The 85 groups plan to submit the joint

stories.title: Who is Viktor Orbán, Hungarian PM fighting to stay in power after 16 years?
stories.summary: Since 2010, Orbán has transformed Hungary into what the European Parliament has denounced as a "hybrid regime of electoral autocracy".

stories.title: Arte: Hungary elections: Is this the end of Orbán?
stories.summary: With elections in other EU countries on the horizon, this election is being seen as a litmus test for Europe.

stories.title: Arte: A magyar választások eredménye indikátor lehet az EU jövőjére nézve
stories.summary: A magyar választásokra úgy tekintenek külföldön, mint a jobboldali populizmus lakmuszpapírjára.

stories.title: Magyar Péter Miskolcon már a győzelemre készítette híveit, az ellenfeleit pedig a választási csalástól akarta elrettenteni
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt miniszterelnök-jelöltjét fáklyás tömeg várta a borsodi megyeszékhelyen. Sokan tőle remélik, hogy hatalomra kerülve segít visszacsábítani az elvándorló fiatalokat, rendbe hozza a térség rossz állapotú úthálózatát és javítani fogja az egészségügyi ellátás színvonalát.

stories.title: Erdély és Magyarország kívülről: külföldön élő erdélyi magyarok a választásról
stories.summary: Nem Erdélyben élnek, és nem is Magyarországon, de továbbra is kötődnek mindkettőhöz. Van, aki hajnalban indul, hogy beálljon a konzulátus előtt kígyózó sorba, más levélben adja le a voksát, és olyan is akad, aki inkább kimarad. Megnéztük, hogyan látják a választást azok, akik távolról figyelik az eseményeket: számít-e még nekik, és ha igen, miért.

stories.title: Húsz év után valódi ellenfélre lelt Orbán Viktor
stories.summary: A Fidesz két éve próbál fogást találni Magyar Péteren, sikertelenül. A legfrissebb mérések szerint a Tisza kétharmados többséggel győzhet a választáson. Videós összefoglalónkban megmutatjuk, hogyan jutottunk ide.

stories.title: Inflációra panaszkodnak a magyarok, de rekordpénz parkol a számláikon
stories.summary: Folyamatosan emelkedik a lakossági folyószámlákon lekötés nélkül tartott pénz mennyisége, amire a választás előtti pénzeső még rá is erősített – derül ki a BiztosDöntés.hu által összegyűjtött adatokból. Mindez annak ellenére történik így, hogy nem is olyan régen még két számjegyű volt az infláció Magyarországon, és az egyik legegyszerűbb megtakarítási terméknek tekinthető lekötött betéteknél is találni versenyképes ajánlatokat a bankok kínálatában.

stories.title: „már készülünk is a rendszerváltásra, de nem tudjuk előre, bőrönd lesz-e belőle vagy válltáska”
stories.summary: Közéleti versek a Telexen! Három magyar szerzőt kértünk fel arra, hogy a költészet napja alkalmából írjanak verset a hazai közéletre reagálva. Harmadikként Háy János alkotását közöljük.

stories.title: Addig rajzolgatták a választási térképet, amíg sikerült egy hatfős budai szavazókört alkotni
stories.summary: A Nemzeti Választási Iroda szerint a Budaörsi út melletti szavazóficak „még ha furcsának is tűnik, így törvényes”.

stories.title: Melania Trump váratlanul ismét felhozta az Epstein-ügyet, ami az elnöknek sem jön jól
stories.summary: Megy a találgatás arról, hogy miért épp most szólalt meg a first lady a botrányról, amely miatt korábban az elnöknek már magyarázkodnia kellett.

stories.title: „Kérem, telepítsen magyar fegyveres erőket szuverén területünkre” – itt van a csádi elnök Orbánnak küldött 2023-as levele
stories.summary: Akár fegyveres terrorista csoportokkal szembeni közvetlen küzdelemre is bevetette volna a magyar katonákat a csádi elnök, ha megvalósul a honvédség csádi missziója. Orbánnak küldött levele szerint „a magyar fegyveres erőknek jogukban fog állni erőt alkalmazni, beleértve szükség esetén a halálos erőt is.”

stories.title: A dolgok nem néznek ki jól, igazságtalan évek jöhetnek – Orbán a kampányzáróján
stories.summary: Ki tud több embert megmozgatni, és mi lesz az utolsó üzenetük a válásztás előtti utolsó napon? Élőben közvetítjük Magyar Péter és Orbán kampányzáróját, a stúdióban pedig Bódi Mátyással elemzünk.

stories.title: Sérültet mentett a barlangi mentős a Mátyás-hegyen, hazafelé tartott, amikor előtte zuhant le az autó a Göncz Árpád városközpont felüljárójáról
stories.summary: A Magyar Barlangi Mentőszolgálat riasztásvezetője a Mátyás-hegyi-barlangból tartott haza, amikor közvetlenül előtte zuhant le az autó a felüljáróról. A mentők és a tűzoltók kiérkezéséig megkezdte a súlyos sérült újraélesztését.

stories.title: Hogyan lettek „élet-halál urai” a fideszes képviselők a területfejlesztésben?
stories.summary: A központosítás nyomán az országgyűlési képviselők vették át a területfejlesztés irányítását az önkormányzatoktól az elmúlt évtizedben, ami növelte a politikai befolyást és a korrupciós kockázatokat.

stories.title: Dúró Dóra szerint minden eddiginél nagyobb esély van arra, hogy a nemzeti radikalizmus megkerülhetetlen tényező legyen a parlamentben
stories.summary: Budapesten zárta szombaton a kampányát a Mi Hazánk Mozgalom.

stories.title: Political Capital: Magyarország helye a világban hosszú időre eldőlhet
stories.summary: Az elemzőintézet szerint ha az Orbán-rezsim kormányon marad, belátható időn belül búcsút inthet az ország az Európai Uniónak és a nyugati szövetségi rendszernek.

stories.title: Even if Victor Orbán is ousted on Sunday, Hungary’s return to liberal democracy is not guaranteed | Gabriela Greilinger and Cas Mudde
stories.summary: Challenger Péter Magyar is no progressive – and after 16 years of creeping authoritarianism, the PM has embedded Fidesz in the Hungarian state On Sunday, Hungarians will go to the polls to decide on their country’s direction for the next four years in an election that looks as if it will be a nail-biter. Viktor Orbán, Europe’s longest-serving prime minister – who has been in power for 16 years and transformed his country into an electoral autocracy – could lose the election . Ahead of the vote,

stories.title: Eddig 113 ezer levélszavazat érkezett be Romániából és 46 ezer Szerbiából
stories.summary: A többi országból jóval kevesebb, Németországból például hétezer, Nagy-Britanniából kevesebb mint háromezer.

stories.title: Toroczkai László: Ma már kiderült, hogy hazudott Magyar Péter
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke nemrég azt állította, hallotta, hogy a Mi Hazánk több tucat képviselőjelöltje fog visszalépni a Fidesz javára. Toroczkai szerint ez nem jött be.

stories.title: A becsület nem üres szó, hanem azt jelenti, hogy kiállunk az igazság mellett, akkor is, amikor nehéz
stories.summary: „Megtisztelő, hogy önöket szolgálhattam” – mondta a Rendszerbontó Nagykoncerten Pálinkás Szilveszter, aki talán utoljára lépett egyenruhában a nyilvánosság elé. „Ebben a rendszerben alkotmányos alapjogaink se tudnak érvényesülni, ennek véget kell vetni” – mondta Szabó Bence rendőrszázados.

stories.title: Fact-check: Tényleg 48 ezer forintot spórol havonta egy magyar család a védett benzináron?
stories.summary: A benzinárstop védelmében közölt adatok szerint évente 60 ezer kilométert autózik egy család, ami több, ami egy keményen hajtó reptéri taxisnak is becsületére válna.

stories.title: „Ki fogja magából dobni a magyar társadalom teste ezt a NER nevű sejtet”
stories.summary: „Ruszkik haza! Mocskos Fidesz!” – zúgott a hatórás Rendszerbontó Nagykoncerten. Az előadók úgy érezték, feladatuk fellépni ezen a koncerten, és bíznak a kormányváltásban. Van, aki szerint a zeneipar 90 százalékának elege van a Fideszből.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Ez itt a mi hazánk, holnap visszavesszük ezt a gyönyörű országot
stories.summary: A Fidesz a Szentháromság téren tartotta a kampányzáróját, Orbán szerint legenda és kamu, hogy a fiatalok ellenük vannak. Magyar Péter azt mondta a Tisza debreceni kampányzáróján, hogy kivizsgálják az orosz befolyást és vizsgálatot indítanak Szijjártó Péter ellen. A párhuzamos kampányzárók és a kampány utolsó napja percről percre.

stories.title: Poland: "Frog Patrol" saves thousands of amphibians during migration
stories.summary: Volunteers near Warsaw form a frog patrol each spring, helping thousands of frogs and toads cross a road safely and protect local breeding grounds.

stories.title: Elections in Hungary: What do the polls say?
stories.summary: Medián, currently considered among the most accurate pollsters in Hungary, predicts a two-thirds majority in parliament for opposition party Tisza. Most other pollsters estimate a smaller lead for them, while few have the ruling Fidesz party narrowly ahead.

stories.title: Fact check: Orban points to Ukraine over pipeline bomb
stories.summary: Orban strongly suggests explosives found near a key gas pipeline in Serbia are evidence of Ukrainian sabotage. Serbia's intelligence chief publicly disagrees.

stories.title: Hungary election: Voters want new EU approach, but tough Ukraine stance, poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll ahead of Hungary's election suggests that most voters want at least minor changes to ties with the EU. But it also found skepticism of financial support for Ukraine, and voters prioritizing domestic issues.

stories.title: A new generation of politicians of colour is emerging in France. The backlash speaks volumes
stories.summary: Local elections have led to a surge of racism in a country that still struggles to see itself as anything other than white Saint-Denis is just over 9km from the centre of Paris but is in the poorest department in all of metropolitan France, a region marked by unemployment, low incomes and social disadvantage. But Saint Denis’s town hall was the backdrop to memorably joyous celebrations on the evening of 15 March. A delirious crowd carried the new mayor shoulder high, chanting his name over and o

stories.title: Virtual Romanian singer goes viral but sparks criticism
stories.summary: In Romania, an AI-generated singer inspired by poems about the Roma community has become an overnight success. But critics say her creator is monetizing the woes of a marginalized group without involving its members.

stories.title: Hungarians living in Ukraine caught between front lines
stories.summary: About 80,000 ethnic Hungarians live in western Ukraine. Many feel torn between Viktor Orban's anti-Ukrainian campaign and the conflict between the two countries. What does the future hold for this minority?

stories.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

stories.title: US and Iran begin direct talks in Pakistan
stories.summary: The U.S. and Iran began direct trilateral talks with Pakistan on Saturday to end the war in the Middle East, according to media reports citing American and Iranian officials.  Delegations from Iran and the U.S. traveled to Islamabad on Saturday to hold negotiations on a peace deal after Washington and Tehran reached a fragile ceasefire to the Middle East war earlier this week. The American and Iranian delegations met separately with Pakistani mediators before starting trilateral talks that

stories.title: Trump endorses ‘focused’ immigration enforcement funding bill
stories.summary: President Donald Trump gave his blessing Friday afternoon for a party-line package focused narrowly on immigration enforcement — in a boost to Senate GOP leaders amid the Department of Homeland Security funding stalemate. Trump’s comments came after he met Friday with Senate Budget Chair Lindsey Graham of South Carolina and Senate Majority Whip John Barrasso of Wyoming. The two lawmakers went to the White House to pitch Senate GOP leadership’s plan to restrict the party’s fil

stories.title: UK puts Diego Garcia handover on hold to appease Trump
stories.summary: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s government is setting aside a plan to hand over the Chagos Islands to Mauritius because of opposition from U.S. President Donald Trump’s administration, according to British media reports.  A bill underpinning the deal will not be included in the King’s Speech next month, The Times and other outlets reported on Saturday. POLITICO reported in February that Starmer’s administration would “pause for thought” on the Chagos plan. The biggest island

stories.title: Trump reportedly says he’ll issue mass pardons at end of his presidential term
stories.summary: President already has issued sweeping pardons throughout second term, including for 1,500 US Capital riot defendants Donald Trump has reportedly said he will issue pardons en masse to his closest advisers at the end of his second presidency, promising them in casual conversations over the last year. “I’ll pardon everyone who has come within 200 feet of the Oval [Office],” the president reportedly said in a recent meeting, garnering laughs from the room, according to a Wall Street Journal report

stories.title: Attacker in New York subway machete attack shot and killed by police
stories.summary: Three people wounded in attack at Grand Central subway station by man who stated he was ‘Lucifer’, police said Police in New York City shot and killed a man who stabbed three people on a subway platform in New York City ’s Grand Central station, the city’s police commissioner said. Jessica Tisch, who leads the New York police department, told reporters at a news conference at the station that officers, flagged down by a witness to the stabbings at about 9.40am, had encountered a suspect, armed w

stories.title: More than 200 people arrested at Palestine Action protest in London
stories.summary: Arrests and detentions took place at first mass demo since group’s ban was ruled unlawful by high court More than 200 people have been arrested at the first mass demonstration opposing the proscription of Palestine Action since the group’s ban was ruled unlawful by the high court. Hundreds of people gathered in Trafalgar Square in London and presented signs reading: “I oppose genocide. I support Palestine Action.” Hundreds of demonstrators sat on camping chairs and on the ground as they held up

stories.title: French man charged with keeping nine-year-old son locked in van since 2024
stories.summary: Police rescued boy after neighbour reported sounds of a child coming from vehicle in Hagenbach in eastern France A malnourished nine-year-old boy was rescued after being locked in his father’s van since 2024 in eastern France, a prosecutor said. A neighbour alerted police to “sounds of a child” coming from a vehicle in the village of Hagenbach, near the borders of Switzerland and Germany. Continue reading...

stories.title: L’Arc de Trump: Plans unveiled for 250-foot arch in Washington
stories.summary: The Trump administration Friday unveiled new renderings of its proposed triumphal arch, a 250-foot structure planned to tower over a traffic circle near Arlington National Cemetery. The memorial, dubbed “Independence Arch,” is part of President Donald Trump’s broader effort to leave a mark on the nation’s capitol and to commemorate the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence this year. “The Triumphal Arch in Memorial Circle is going to be one of the most iconic landma

stories.title: Ukraine and Russia swap 175 war prisoners as Easter ceasefire starts
stories.summary: Ukraine and Russia swapped 175 prisoners of war each on Saturday as a 32-hour ceasefire began on the occasion of Orthodox Easter. Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy posted pictures of the Ukrainian soldiers being welcomed back home. The group includes troops who have fought on different war fronts, from Mariupol to the Donetsk, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia regions. Seven civilians were included. “Some of them are wounded. Most have been in captivity since 2022. And finally — they are home,” Zel

stories.title: UK starts crackdown on EU citizens’ post-Brexit rights
stories.summary: Concerns raised over use of travel data in determining if people are ‘continuously’ in Britain after HMRC fiasco UK ministers are to start removing post-Brexit residency rights from EU citizens who are no longer “continuously” living in the country. The initiative is legal under the 2020 Brexit withdrawal agreement, but the decision to use travel data to partly determine absences has raised concerns after the HMRC fiasco in which almost 20,000 parents were stripped of child benefits because of i

stories.title: Tories would reinstate two-child benefit cap to fund defence, says Badenoch
stories.summary: Conservative leader promises biggest peacetime rearmament effort in UK history if her party is re-elected The Conservatives would reinstate the two-child benefit cap and use the savings for a wide-ranging spending splurge on defence in what Kemi Badenoch said would be “the biggest peacetime programme of rearmament in our country’s history”. Speaking at a defence conference in London, the Tory leader criticised the government for Britain’s “lack of readiness” for war, which has been exposed by re

stories.title: Louisiana Republicans move to eliminate court office won by exonerated man
stories.summary: After Calvin Duncan served 28 years for a murder he didn’t commit, he won an election to serve as criminal court clerk. But now the office might be shut down A man imprisoned for nearly 30 years before being exonerated won a landmark election in New Orleans promising to fix a judicial system that failed him. Now, Louisiana’s governor, Jeff Landry, and the Republican-controlled state legislature are racing to eliminate his job before he can be sworn in. Calvin Duncan won 68% of the vote last Nove

stories.title: UK forced to shelve Chagos Islands legislation after US dropped support
stories.summary: Officials accept that time has run out to pass law to allow transfer of islands to Mauritius The UK government has been forced to shelve its legislation to hand the Chagos Islands to Mauritius, after the US dropped its support for the agreement. On Friday, UK government officials acknowledged that they had run out of time to pass legislation within the current parliamentary session, which ends in the coming weeks. Continue reading...

stories.title: US state department revokes green cards of three Iranian nationals it links to regime
stories.summary: Three arrested by federal agents had family ties to Iranian military general, regime spokesperson or security chief United States federal agents arrested three Iranian nationals – including the son of a revolutionary at the center of the 1979 Iran hostage crisis – after the US state department terminated their green cards, the department announced on Saturday. State department officials revoked the green card status of Seyed Eissa Hashemi, whose mother was an Iranian revolutionary who served as

stories.title: UK freezes deal to return Chagos Islands to Mauritius
stories.summary: A fallout with President Trump has seen the planned handing over of the islands put on ice. At the center of the debate is the use of a joint UK-US military base.

stories.title: Fuel tankers regain access to Irish oil refinery
stories.summary: Demonstrations due to high prices caused by the US-Israeli war against Iran are affecting traffic on several roads.

stories.title: Surging gas prices drive US inflation to highest level in 2 years
stories.summary: Inflation in the U.S. shot up to its highest level in two years in March, and consumer sentiment is at a record low. As the effects of President Donald Trump’s war with Iran ripple across the economy, the combination of surging prices and grim attitudes is a warning sign to Republicans seeking to maintain control of Congress in the midterm elections. Oil and gas prices exploded in March as global energy markets absorbed a once-in-a-generation supply shock from the war with Iran. The halting of t

stories.title: Is Iran Trump’s Suez crisis, or just a passing thunderstorm?
stories.summary: Britain’s standing in the world was never the same after its assault on Egypt in 1956. Now the US risks repeating history in the Middle East Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump’s addiction to framing every event in the most apocalyptic terms is what allows conservative commentators such as Mark Levin to praise him as “a once-in-a-century president”. But Trump cannot play out his entire presidency on a reckless high wire without eventually falling off – potentially taking America with

stories.title: Albanese didn’t return with shiploads of diesel. That doesn’t mean his Singapore visit wasn’t a success
stories.summary: Having received assurances from Singapore over refined fuels, diesel supply will surely be next on the prime minister’s agenda Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese isn’t coming back from Singapore with a shipload of diesel in his checked baggage. That doesn’t mean his whistle-stop visit wasn’t a success, or that it won’t be seen in future as a pivotal moment if fuel stocks continue to be choked by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The government ne

stories.title: Jubilant return of Artemis II shadowed by ‘extinction-level’ cuts to Nasa: ‘It’s discordant’
stories.summary: Even as a triumphant moon flyby primes agency for a 2028 landing, Trump’s proposed budget cuts cast pall on US space program The astronauts on board Artemis II were “almost poets”, Nasa’s administrator, Jared Isaacman, declared on Friday, referring to their inspiring words as they swung above the lunar surface. They were, he said, “ambassadors for humanity” as they became the first humans to travel to the moon and return safely to Earth since 1972, on a mission that broke a distance record . Con

stories.title: US man in Bahamian jail after wife disappears into Atlantic waters during boat trip
stories.summary: Lynette and Brian Hooker, from Michigan, were years into a sailing adventure when Brian said his wife fell overboard Lynette Hooker bounced around the deck of the docked Soul Mate, smiled into the camera and proclaimed, “We’re finally leaving Kemah,” referring to a Texas port town. “It’s only been four months,” she said as her husband, Brian, tugged on some rigging as they got ready to set sail. Continue reading...

stories.title: Woman, 19, dies after being attacked by dog at property in Essex
stories.summary: Police arrest man on suspicion of being in charge of a dog dangerously out of control and causing injury resulting in death A 19-year-old woman has died after being attacked by a dog at a property in Essex. Police have arrested a 37-year-old man on suspicion of being in charge of a dog dangerously out of control and causing injury resulting in death, after the incident on Friday. He is now in police custody. Continue reading...

stories.title: Why the Nato alliance is not as likely to dissolve as Trump makes it seem
stories.summary: Trump has upped the stridency and threatening nature of his complaints, but the US and Europe need each other Collateral damage is a universally acknowledged hazard of war – more commonly known for its impact on truth and non-combatant civilians. Its consequences are much less frequently visited on military alliances. Continue reading...

stories.title: US and Iran hold talks in Islamabad as Pakistan seeks to broker peace deal
stories.summary: JD Vance leads American delegation while Iran’s negotiators headed up by Iran’s parliamentary speaker Peace talks between Iran and the US began in Islamabad this afternoon, with senior negotiators from both countries meeting face to face at the highest level for the first time since 1979, in the presence of mediators from Pakistan. Pakistani state TV said US and Iranian officials were “sitting directly at the same table” – which was later confirmed by the White House – and discussions were begin

stories.title: JD Vance dispatched to negotiate Iran peace with few cards to play
stories.summary: Vice-president’s war doubts and his boss’s desperation to reopen the Hormuz strait constitute a weak deck against bolstered opponents Middle East crisis – live updates As JD Vance arrives in Islamabad to negotiate a peace deal with Iran, his first high-profile assignment of the war looks to be a poisoned chalice. Vance, a vocal opponent of US wars in the Middle East gone quiet since the beginning of the current military campaign, will now face off with Iranian negotiators who feel emboldened by

stories.summary: Exclusive: Move comes after Guardian Australia revealed Gemma Seymour was facing potential suspension over video criticising RMIT’s ties to weapons companies RMIT University has dropped a misconduct case against a student who accused the institution of being “complicit in genocide” in Gaza , because of its defence and aerospace research centre’s ties to weapons companies. stories.title: Trump says plans filed for huge triumphal arch in Washington
stories.summary: The US arch would be over 50% taller than the famous Arc de Triomphe in Paris, according to the official plans. The US president has described the project as "the GREATEST and MOST BEAUTIFUL Triumphal Arch" in the world.

stories.title: India news: Delhi looks to ban fossil fuel two-wheelers from 2028
stories.summary: India's capital is gearing up to ban non-electric two-wheelers as it looks to tackle chronic smog and high pollution. In other news, PM Modi is campaigning in West Bengal ahead of high-stake elections.

stories.title: Germany news: Coalition split widens over fuel price relief
stories.summary: There's trouble at the top of Europe's largest economy as a dispute over fuel price relief for consumers intensifies. Meanwhile, figures appear to show a significant rise in the number of rapes. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Ukraine: Attacks continue as Orthodox Easter ceasefire nears
stories.summary: Russia has continued to pound Ukraine in the hours leading up to a 36-hour pause in its war of aggression. Three Ukrainians were killed overnight and dozens were injured in drone attacks on Odesa, Poltava and Sumy.

stories.title: UK: Sudanese man held over migrant drownings in Channel
stories.summary: UK authorities have detained a 27-year-old man in the aftermath of migrant drownings in the English Channel. Four people died on Thursday attempting to reach the UK from France.

stories.title: Is this Syrian doctor one of the 80% Germany wants to remove?
stories.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has ignited a debate by declaring that 80% of Syrians would be repatriated. Does this mean that Germany also intends to deport those who are integrated and whom the country needs?

stories.title: Ukrainian civilians abducted, tortured in Russian prisons
stories.summary: Russia has been holding thousands of Ukrainian civilians behind bars for years, in breach of international humanitarian law. Families have been fighting for their loved ones to be released, some for more than a decade.

stories.title: Germans see little progress on cutting red tape
stories.summary: Most people and businesses in Germany say they've seen little change in bureaucracy despite Chancellor Friedrich Merz's government promises to reduce it.

stories.title: Russian Memorial human rights NGO labeled 'extremist'
stories.summary: Russia's Supreme Court has labeled the human rights organization Memorial as "extremist." Experts say this represents a new level of repression in Russia.

stories.title: Germany: Lufthansa pilots' union call for two-day strike
stories.summary: The pilot's union said Lufthansa had shown "no discernible willingness to reach a solution" in several collective bargaining disputes.

stories.title: Artemis crew home safely after completing historic mission to the Moon
stories.summary: Four astronauts splashed down in the Pacific after a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans.

stories.title: Baseball statue broken as it is unveiled in Seattle
stories.summary: The mishap occurred as a tarp was pulled off a bronze figure immortalising Hall of Famer Ichiro Suzuki.

stories.title: 'You are my god', Japan's PM tells British rock band Deep Purple
stories.summary: Hard rock enthusiast Sanae Takaichi beamed as she met the rockers in her Tokyo office.

stories.title: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote

stories.title: Four men deported by US to Eswatini have right to see lawyer, court rules
stories.summary: The men, sent to the southern African country in July, have been denied in-person counsel for nine months Four men deported by the US to Eswatini and denied in-person legal counsel for nine months while detained in a maximum security prison have the right to see a local lawyer, Eswatini’s supreme court ruled. stories.title: Venezuela police use tear gas against protesters demanding better pay and pensions
stories.summary: The monthly minimum wage in Venezuela is 130 bolivars (€0.23), unchanged since 2022, and roughly 330 times lower than the UN poverty line of $3 (€2.56) a day.

stories.summary: Russian drone strikes hit Sumy and Odesa, injuring two and damaging homes and energy sites, as Putin proposes a brief Easter ceasefire.

stories.title: Fuel-price protests cause chaos in Ireland and spread to Norway
stories.summary: Irish drivers told to buy only fuel they need as filling stations run out and hauliers and farmers block motorways in fourth day of action Middle East crisis live – latest updates Protests over fuel prices have caused chaos in Ireland and spread to Norway in a knock-on effect from the conflict in the Middle East . stories.summary: Vaianu, forecast to bring heavy rain and winds of up to 130 kmh (80 mph), is expected to hit on Sunday Thousands of New Zealanders were ordered to evacuate their homes on Saturday as the country’s North Island braced for Cyclone Vaianu, which authorities warned could cause coastal flooding and landslides. stories.summary: The four crew members are back on Earth after spending nine days in space and splashing down in the Pacific Ocean off the California coast.

stories.title: Londonban nem lesz tiszás külképviseleti megfigyelő, de fideszes és mi hazánkos kettő is
stories.summary: A Fidesz 42 delegáltjából 39-et, a Tisza 71 jelöltjéből csak 29-et vett nyilvántartásba a választási bizottság, de a legtöbb fontos városban azért így is lesz ellenzéki megfigyelő. Az NVB elárulta, miért utasította el a delegáltakat, mi pedig megmutatjuk, melyik külképviseleteken lesznek jelen az egyes pártok.

stories.summary: A Szentkirályi tulajdonosa a Partizánban arról beszélt, nem a Fidesz elleni szavazásra buzdított a pénteki videójában, de nem ért egyet az Európai Uniótól való eltávolodással.

stories.title: Valós kockázata van, hogy a libanoni helyzet összeomlaszthatja az iráni tűzszünetet, de ez nem történik meg automatikusan
stories.summary: Izrael az elmúlt hetek legnagyobb támadását indította a Hezbollah ellen az iráni tűzszünet bejelentése után, Netanjahu és a Fehér Ház szerint ez két külön hadszíntér. De Trump a támadások visszafogását várja az izraeli kormányfőtől.

stories.title: Autokráciában másként működik a verseny, de ha van tét, fokozódik a választási részvétel
stories.summary: A részvételi adatot befolyásolja, hogy a választók mennyire tartják kiélezettnek a versenyt a pártok és a jelöltek között, de az állami beavatkozás is növeli a szavazók számát.

stories.title: Történelmi űrutazás után sikeresen visszatért a Földre az Artemis-2 legénysége
stories.summary: Ember még nem járt olyan messze a bolygónktól, mint az Orion űrkapszula négy űrhajósa, akik olyan dolgokat láthattak, amit ember még sosem. Ez most inkább teszt volt, mint holdfoglalás, de megalapozhatja a későbbi holdra szállásokat.

stories.title: Nem tudsz sírni? Japánban fizethetsz azért, hogy megríkassanak, majd letöröljék a könnyeidet
stories.summary: Japánban a sírás sokáig tabu volt, aztán jöttek a könnytörölgető szépfiúk, akiknek a szolgáltatásait cégek is igénybe veszik csapatépítő jelleggel.

stories.title: Lezuhant egy autó a felüljáróról a halálos balesetben a Göncz Árpád városközpontnál
stories.summary: Szemtanúk szerint több autó versenyzett, amikor az egyik egy vétlen autóba ütközött, amely lezuhant.

stories.title: Még tartanak az amerikai-iráni tárgyalások, közben áthaladt két amerikai hadihajó a Hormuzi-szoroson
stories.summary: Hírfolyamunk a közel-keleti konfliktusról.

stories.title: Gyanús itallal elkábított nők vádolták meg nemi erőszakkal a demokraták kaliforniai kormányzójelöltjét
stories.summary: Eric Swalwell visszautasította a vádakat, de a Demokrata Párt visszalépésre szólította fel.

stories.title: Ukraine: Russia's Putin declares Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: Russian President Putin has announced a ceasefire in its war in Ukraine over the Orthodox Easter holiday. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy said his country will "act accordingly" during the truce.

stories.title: Lufthansa cabin crew begin strike
stories.summary: Hundreds of flights have been canceled as cabin crew members walk out. This is Lufthansa's third major strike this year.

stories.title: Recycling's next big thing — or big bluff?
stories.summary: An advanced recycling process claims it can handle hard-to-recycle plastics, like packaging. But critics say it's not worth the effort.

stories.title: Germany: Police suspect antisemitism in Israeli restaurant attack in Munich
stories.summary: Police and restaurant owners believe antisemitism motivated the Munich attack. Nobody was hurt, but three windows were broken, causing damage worth several thousand euros. This comes amid tension over the war in Iran.

stories.title: NASA's billion-dollar space race goes into overdrive
stories.summary: NASA's renewed push to get a human mission to the moon and Mars is neither cheap, nor for the faint of heart. But who ends up covering the costs and what does everyone still on Earth get out of all this?

stories.title: Iran war: Rifts in regime bigger threat to ceasefire
stories.summary: As the US and Iran gear up for high-stakes talks, the bigger question is whether Tehran can hold the line amid signs of fractures within the regime.

stories.title: Lebanon gripped by uncertainty over Iran-US ceasefire
stories.summary: Following the latest wave of Israeli attacks, Lebanon faces what the UN calls a "perfect storm." Could the first direct talks between Lebanon and Israel address the humanitarian crisis?

stories.title: Scotland: Abusive man gets 8 years prison for wife's suicide
stories.summary: Lee Milne engaged in physical and psychological abuse of his wife, Kimberly, with the victim then taking her own life. The landmark case saw him convicted of culpable homicide.

stories.title: Molotov cocktail thrown at Sam Altman's home, man arrested
stories.summary: The attack occurred at the OpenAI CEO's home in San Francisco early on Friday. OpenAI said no one was hurt in the attack and thanked city police for their response.

stories.title: Melania Trump: 'I never had a relationship with Epstein'
stories.summary: Watch the first lady's full statement from the White House as she addresses her links to the late sex offender.

stories.title: US First Lady Melania Trump says 'I was not a participant' in Epstein sexual abuse
stories.summary: Epstein died in federal custody in 2019 while awaiting trial on sex trafficking charges involving minors, but the scandal has repeatedly overshadowed Trump's second presidency.

stories.title: Russia's president announces Orthodox Easter ceasefire with Ukraine, Kremlin says
stories.summary: Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy had previously signalled Kyiv's openness to a potential Easter truce.

stories.title: Former Israeli PM: US-Israel-Iran war ceasefire – what next?
stories.summary: In an opinion piece for Euronews, former Israeli Prime Minister Ehud Olmert says the central objectives of the war were not met: the regime did not collapse, enriched uranium remains in Iran’s hands, and it still dominates the Strait of Hormuz. But despite this, there is still a chance for peace.

stories.title: A 'glass-like' battlefield: German Army chief on the future of warfare
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview with Euronews, German Army chief Christian Freuding outlines how data, speed and digital systems are reshaping modern warfare.

stories.title: Lebanon reeling as Israeli strikes continue after 300 killed yesterday
stories.summary: Israeli air strikes hit Hezbollah sites in southern Lebanon as rocket sirens blare in northern Israel, despite calls to extend a regional ceasefire.

stories.title: Brazil: Indigenous marches in Brasília enter third day over land rights
stories.summary: Thousands of Indigenous people marched through Brasília on 9 April 2026 during the 22nd “Free Land Encampment”, urging authorities to recognise ancestral lands ahead of October’s elections.

stories.title: Putin announces Easter ceasefire, proposed by Kyiv for weeks
stories.summary: Moscow repeatedly ignored Kyiv’s calls for Easter weekend ceasefire around 12 April just to announce what it called its own “unilateral" truce.

stories.title: Pakistan prepares to host US-Iran talks as ceasefire remains shaky
stories.summary: Islamabad is getting ready to host talks on Friday between the United States and Iran on a resolution to the war and the Strait of Hormuz, which remains at a standstill despite the ceasefire.

stories.title: Pentagon denies threatening Vatican over pope's remarks on US intervention in Iran
stories.summary: Pentagon and Vatican envoys have both denied reports that US officials gave the Holy See's ambassador to the US a "bitter lecture," demanding the Church align with the Trump administration over Pope Leo XIV's criticism of the Iran war.

stories.title: Overlooked IRGC institution holds key to Iran's Hormuz approach, expert warns
stories.summary: The fragile US-Iran ceasefire is already faltering, with Tehran still blocking the Strait of Hormuz navigation in protest against Israel’s attacks in Lebanon. A leading Iran expert indicates Tehran's Hormuz strategy could be part of a well-established strategy.

stories.title: Israeli restaurant targeted in suspected antisemitic attack in Munich
stories.summary: Antisemitic offences in Germany have risen significantly in recent years, reaching a new peak in 2024. Authorities recorded 6,236 cases, including 173 violent incidents, according to available data.

stories.title: Mr Nobody on leaving Russia behind: Oscar-winning director Pavel Talankin talks home truths
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview, Paval Talankin, winner of the 2026 Best Documentary Oscar, tells Euronews about live outside his Russian homeland and the "discarded" generation for whom he made 'Mr Nobody Against Putin'.

stories.title: Ongoing wars 'not failure of UN but of member states', General Assembly President tells Euronews
stories.summary: The head of the United Nations' main policy-making organ responded to mounting criticism of her organisation in a Euronews interview, arguing the abuse of vetoes by key members is blocking progress.

stories.title: Man accused of coercing wife into sex with 120 men goes on trial in Sweden
stories.summary: The 61-year-old denies threatening physical violence and plying his wife with drugs to force her into having sex with strangers.

stories.title: Former hostage In Iran faces surreal tax troubles in France after release
stories.summary: Former French hostage in Iran, Benjamin Brière, said his release did not end his ordeal, as he struggled with taxes, benefits and reintegration back home.

stories.title: Emmanuel Macron meets Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican to discuss global tensions
stories.summary: France’s President Emmanuel Macron met Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican on Friday, marking their first official talks and signalling renewed dialogue between France and the Holy See at a time of rising global tensions.

stories.title: Israel: Hezbollah rocket from Lebanon strikes Safed, injuring several people
stories.summary: Emergency services were deployed in Safed on 10 April 2026 after a rocket fired from Lebanon by Hezbollah struck the northern city, prompting a rapid response to treat casualties and secure the area.

stories.title: Chimpanzees in Uganda locked in vicious 'civil war', say researchers
stories.summary: The once close-knit community of Ngogo chimpanzees have been at loggerheads for the last eight years.

stories.title: Italy: From Go to Monopoly, board games bridge generations
stories.summary: A new exhibition titled “0–99. Design for Play” opened on 10 April 2026 at Palazzo Arese Borromeo in Cesano Maderno, near Milan, turning a 17th‑century aristocratic residence into a large, interactive gallery of games.

stories.title: Palestinian shot dead during Israeli settler attack on occupied West Bank village
stories.summary: The killing came as ex-security chiefs warned "government sponsored Jewish terrorism" was out of control.

stories.title: South Korea deploys thermal imaging cameras in search for escaped zoo wolf
stories.summary: The male wolf, named Neukgu, escaped a zoo in the central city of Daejeon on Wednesday, officials said.

stories.title: The five big sticking points in US-Iran talks
stories.summary: Peace talks between the US and Iran are due to begin in Pakistan on Saturday.

stories.title: JD Vance warns Iran not to 'play' US during planned talks in Pakistan
stories.summary: The US vice president said he was "looking forward to negotiations" in Pakistan as UK Prime Minister Starmer classified the ceasefire as "fragile".

stories.title: A day in the life of a 19-year-old in ICE detention: ‘I feel that this nightmare is not going to end’
stories.summary: Olivia has been detained for months at the sprawling Dilley center in Texas. She has lost 20lb, and wakes up every day with a headache Each day in detention feels like 48 hours for Olivia. The 19-year-old asylum seeker from the Democratic Republic of the Congo has been at the Dilley Immigration processing center in Texas for more than four months. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump criticizes NATO over Iran in meeting with Rutte
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said NATO "wasn't there" after the US and Israel launched attacks on Iran. But NATO head Mark Rutte said "a large majority of European countries" lived up to their side of the deal.

stories.title: Ten years after Brexit, this is the UK: a divided nation frozen in time | Aditya Chakrabortty
stories.summary: Tribalism has not faded over the past decade. Instead, new research reveals our politics has become ever-more polarised and fractious On 23 June 2016 , the British voter changed. Before that day, they picked a party, usually red or blue. By that morning, only two tribes mattered: remain or leave. And they kept mattering long, long after the result was declared. Rather than bin those short-lived and now stale allegiances, voters made them their personas. No longer a “Labour man” or a “Conservativ

stories.title: US accused of pressuring Latin America to cut ties with Cuban doctors program
stories.summary: Cuba accuses US of ‘extorting’ countries in pushing them to axe deals with Havana to send doctors on medical missions Cuba’s foreign minister has accused the United States of “extorting” Latin American countries by putting pressure on them to cancel decades-old deals with Havana for the supply of doctors. Bruno Rodríguez said the United States was trying to “strangle” the economy of the communist island, which earns billions from its foreign medical missions, after several countries stopped depl
